global_context.scene_description: A photorealistic Phoenix residential street scene showing two HVAC service trucks parked at the curb in front of a Southwestern stucco home. The truck on the LEFT is a large, polished, glossy white corporate-style box truck with a tall enclosed cargo area and a mounted ladder rack — generic, no readable logo or branding, but it has the clean over-styled look of a private-equity-backed company fleet truck. The truck on the RIGHT is a smaller, more practical white work van with a single rear ladder and a simpler clean profile — generic, no readable logo, but it has the look of a local family-owned shop. Same neighborhood, same time of day, same lighting. The houses behind both trucks are typical Phoenix flat-roof stucco homes with desert landscaping. Late afternoon golden Phoenix light bathes both trucks evenly. The composition is wide, shot from across the street, slight angle showing both trucks in profile. The contrast in scale between the two trucks is the visual story. Top 18% of frame is reserved for headline overlay. Bottom 14% of frame is reserved for red CTA banner overlay. Mood: quiet contrast, side-by-side decision.
global_context.time_of_day: late afternoon, golden hour Phoenix light

lighting.source: low golden sun from camera-right, slightly behind
lighting.direction: raking from right, casting long soft shadows

composition.camera_angle: ground-level, slight 3/4 angle, from across the street
composition.framing: wide, both trucks fully visible side-by-side
composition.depth_of_field: deep, both trucks and house background sharp
composition.focal_point: the contrast in scale between the two trucks
composition.notes: top 18% reserved for headline overlay (clear sky); bottom 14% reserved for red CTA banner overlay (street/sidewalk in foreground)

no logos on either truck — keeps the comparison about category, not specific brand callout
